more.title: How the Skeleton of Mutapa Times is Based on Sarah Charlesworth's Work
more.description: Conceptual artist Sarah Charlesworth's 1979 study of how newspapers covered a solar eclipse inspired the framework behind The Mutapa Times.

more.title: Mnangagwa Launches US$5 Million Munhumutapa Challenge Cup in Harare
more.description: HARARE – President Emmerson Mnangagwa has officially launched the US$5 million-a-year ZIFA Munhumutapa Challenge Cup, positioning it as Zimbabwe’s richest domestic football tournament. The English FA Cup-style competition was unveiled at the Celebration Centre in Borrowdale, Harare, in a ceremony attended by senior government officials, football administrators and corporate sponsors. Over five years, the tournament [...]The post Mnangagwa Launches US$5 Million Munhumutapa Challenge Cup in Harare appeared first on The Zimbabwe Mail.

more.title: Bad news for Mike Chimombe and Moses Mpofu, to spend more years in jail as deadline passes
more.description: HARARE – The saga surrounding prominent businessmen Moses Mpofu and Mike Chimombe continues to unfold, as a recent deadline for the restitution of US$3 million to the Zimbabwean government passed without payment.

more.title: Oil Prices Surge Past $85 as Strait of Hormuz Tensions Rattle Global Markets
more.description: Global energy and financial markets were thrown into turmoil this week as escalating tensions in the Middle East drove oil prices sharply higher and triggered declines across major stock exchanges. Brent crude futures climbed above $85 per barrel for the first time since mid-2024, amid fears that the strategic Strait of Hormuz could be closed.
